item.title: Apple’s smooth CEO swap is the ultimate corporate status symbol
item.description: There are two kinds of companies — those that can do CEO transitions without intense upheaval and those that can’t

item.title: UniCredit treads fine line between activism and M&A
item.description: Italian bank’s cage-rattling is best understood in the right context

item.title: Oil market has lost a billion barrels due to Iran war, Vitol boss warns
item.description: Top energy traders warn of unprecedented hit to global energy supplies

item.title: CATL claims 6-minute charge and 1,500km range for new electric vehicle batteries
item.description: Chinese group also slashes charging time in race against BYD for electric vehicle battery supremacy

item.title: VW to export more China-made EVs in global sales drive
item.description: Strategy pivot coincides with slowdown and fierce competition in world’s largest automotive market

item.title: Shell faces fresh climate court case
item.description: Dutch non-profit activist group makes push to halt new oil and gas production

item.title: Reader callout: are you going solar to avoid the energy crunch?
item.description: FT Money wants to hear from readers with solar panels — or considering them — as an answer to rising bills

item.title: Ex-Swedbank chief cleared of conviction in money-laundering scandal
item.description: Birgitte Bonnesen had faced 11-month prison sentence for ‘gross swindling’

item.title: Royal Mail reduces second-class post service in £500mn overhaul
item.description: Cheaper deliveries will move to every other weekday and will be scrapped on Saturdays

item.title: UnitedHealth boosts 2026 earnings guidance after increasing prices
item.description: US healthcare group steadies itself after turbulent period that led to departure of previous CEO

item.title: Revolut aims for $200bn valuation in stock market listing
item.description: Bumper IPO would trigger increased stake for founder Nik Storonsky but group has no plans to float shares before 2028

item.title: Europe’s AI endgame must bet on reliability
item.description: If the region fails to lead on safe and secure AI, it risks remaining stuck on the wrong side of the tech wall

item.title: How the energy shock has reshaped jet fuel economics
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter, environmental groups sue to block BP’s $5bn deepwater drilling project

item.title: Iran war spurs global demand for defence equipment, says Thales
item.description: French group expects Middle East conflict to boost orders, from rockets to air surveillance systems

item.title: EU countries struggle to replenish gas reserves for winter
item.description: Iran war keeps prices high despite consumer demand dropping as heating season ends

item.title: Home Depot courts contractors as US economy weighs on the DIY set
item.description: Home improvement giant spends billions to attract new business from professionals beyond traditional customer base

item.title: Ofcom to investigate Telegram over child sexual abuse material allegations
item.description: Two teen chat sites also being probed by watchdog

item.title: FirstFT: Apple picks Tim Cook’s successor
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Kevin Warsh’s confirmation hearings begin and rebuilding Gaza

item.title: Crest Nicholson shares slump as housebuilder slashes earnings forecast
item.description: Housebuilder says ‘macro-uncertainty’ fuelled by war in the Middle East will push up cost pressures and damage consumer confidence

item.title: Refinitiv to delete profiles of politicians’ grandchildren after lawsuit
item.description: Minors sued data provider owned by London Stock Exchange Group over listing of relatives of ‘politically exposed persons’

item.title: Apple’s next chief Ternus faces defining AI moment
item.description: Tim Cook’s replacement must lead iPhone-maker through industry shift

item.title: Smartphones to be banned in schools in England
item.description: Government bows to pressure from teachers, parents and Conservative Party

item.title: Miliband unveils move to delink UK gas and electricity prices
item.description: Energy secretary’s push comes as Iran war threatens Labour pledge to cut heating bills

item.title: Apple chief Tim Cook to hand over to John Ternus in September
item.description: Head of hardware to become next chief executive while Cook will become chair of the iPhone maker

item.title: Primark owner ABF confirms plan to demerge fashion chain
item.description: Break-up of conglomerate after 65 years paves way for Primark listing

item.title: Global clean power growth points to permanent shift, analysts say
item.description: Evidence grows of structural move away from fossil fuels to meet electricity demand

item.title: The big short meets private credit
item.description: Wall Street banks have started trading credit default swaps against private credit funds run by Blackstone, Apollo and Ares

item.title: EQT and Omers to lift funding for struggling broadband group to €5bn
item.description: Funding plan comes after investors failed to find new backer to inject cash into debt-laden German provider

item.title: Private credit funds strained as borrowing costs edge higher
item.description: Bond investors and banks are seeking greater premiums on new financing as a precaution before lending

item.title: Will social media addiction go the way of cigarettes?
item.description: Smoking among the rich has declined dramatically — and digital dependency could follow a similar pattern
